title: Schizoid personality disorder
vignette: A 38-year-old man comes to the office for evaluation of discomfort in his wrists. He thinks his symptoms may be related to "typing all day, every day." The patient is a software engineer and prefers to work remotely from home to "avoid wasting time talking and interacting with coworkers." He rarely leaves his apartment and spends his spare time on his computer, reading online forums about space exploration and the possibility of alien life. When asked about relationships he says, "I've always liked being on my own." He spent a large portion of his childhood disassembling and rebuilding electronic devices and had few friends. His affect appears constricted and his responses to questions are limited. Which of the following is the most likely personality disorder in this patient?

clues.text: avoid wasting time talking and interacting with coworkers
clues.note: Genuine disinterest in social interaction (preferring solitude, not fearing rejection) is the core of schizoid personality disorder. He avoids people because he doesn't value the contact, not from anxiety.

clues.text: I've always liked being on my own
clues.note: A long-standing preference for solitary activities and indifference to relationships is the defining feature of schizoid PD. This separates it from avoidant PD, where the person WANTS relationships but fears criticism.

clues.text: affect appears constricted and his responses to questions are limited
clues.note: Constricted/flat affect and emotional detachment fit schizoid PD. Importantly, there are no odd/magical beliefs, perceptual distortions, or eccentric behavior - which would point to schizotypal instead.

whatMatters: A lifelong loner who genuinely PREFERS solitude, is indifferent to relationships, and has constricted affect - without odd beliefs or perceptual distortions - has schizoid personality disorder (Cluster A) - answer E.
strategy: Separate the three 'social-withdrawal' look-alikes: schizoid = doesn't WANT relationships (content alone, flat affect); avoidant = WANTS relationships but avoids from fear of rejection; schizotypal = wants few relationships AND has odd/magical thinking, eccentric behavior, perceptual distortions. Match the reason for withdrawal.

eliminations.label: antisocial - no rights violations
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: disregard for and violation of others' rights (deceit, aggression, lawbreaking), with prior conduct disorder. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: this man harms no one and simply prefers solitude; there's no exploitation or rule-breaking. You chose this. ✅ Correct if: a pattern of deceit, aggression, and lack of remorse were described.

eliminations.label: avoidant - he doesn't desire relationships
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: social inhibition driven by fear of criticism/rejection in someone who DESIRES relationships. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: this patient is content alone and not anxious about rejection - schizoid, not avoidant. ✅ Correct if: he longed for connection but avoided it due to fear of embarrassment.

eliminations.label: obsessive-compulsive PD - wrong theme
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: preoccupation with order, control, perfectionism, and rules. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: there is no perfectionism/rigidity theme here; the issue is social detachment. ✅ Correct if: rigid control, list-making, and inflexibility dominated.

eliminations.label: paranoid - no pervasive distrust
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: pervasive suspiciousness and distrust of others' motives. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: he isn't suspicious or distrustful, just uninterested in people. ✅ Correct if: he interpreted others' actions as malicious/deceptive.

eliminations.label: schizotypal - no odd beliefs/perceptions
eliminations.detail: WHAT IT IS: eccentric behavior with magical thinking, ideas of reference, odd speech, and perceptual distortions. ❌ WHY IT FAILS: his interest in space/alien forums is a normal hobby, not magical thinking or perceptual distortion; he lacks the eccentricity criteria. ✅ Correct if: odd beliefs, ideas of reference, or peculiar speech/appearance were present.

reasoning: Lifelong preference for solitude; indifferent to relationships
Content alone, not fearful of rejection (rules out avoidant)
Constricted affect but NO odd beliefs/perceptual distortions (rules out schizotypal)
Cluster A schizoid personality disorder -> answer E
mechanism: Schizoid personality disorder (DSM-5 Cluster A) is a pervasive pattern of detachment from social relationships and a restricted range of emotional expression: the person neither desires nor enjoys close relationships, chooses solitary activities, has little interest in sexual or interpersonal experiences, appears indifferent to praise or criticism, and shows emotional coldness/constricted affect. It is distinguished from avoidant PD (desires closeness but fears rejection) and from schizotypal PD (adds magical thinking, ideas of reference, and perceptual/behavioral eccentricities). A solitary lifestyle and niche interests alone, without distress or odd cognition, fit schizoid.

difficulty.label: 📚 Pure knowledge gap
difficulty.message: First Aid Psychiatry > Personality disorders: Cluster A = paranoid, schizoid, schizotypal ('weird'); schizoid = voluntary social withdrawal + flat affect, no psychotic-spectrum oddities. Avoidant (Cluster C) = withdrawal from fear of rejection.

difficulty.label: 🧭 Understanding the question
difficulty.message: The discriminating question is WHY he is withdrawn: by preference (schizoid), by fear (avoidant), or with eccentric cognition (schizotypal). The stem says he likes being alone and lacks odd beliefs.

difficulty.label: 🔤 Vocabulary
difficulty.message: Unsure on Cluster A/B/C, schizoid vs schizotypal vs avoidant, constricted affect, ideas of reference, magical thinking? Tell me which.

difficulty.label: 🧲 Pulled by a distractor
difficulty.message: You chose A (antisocial). Antisocial requires VIOLATING others' rights; this man bothers no one. The lonerism-by-choice with flat affect is schizoid.

difficulty.label: ✍️ Something else
difficulty.message: Build the withdrawal triad: schizoid (don't want), avoidant (want but afraid), schizotypal (odd). Match the motive.

keywords.front: Prefers solitude, indifferent to relationships, flat affect, no odd beliefs

keywords.back: Schizoid = doesn't want relationships; avoidant = wants but fears rejection
Schizotypal adds magical thinking, ideas of reference, eccentricity
Violation of others' rights; prior conduct disorder

firstAid.detail: Psychiatry: Cluster A, social detachment, restricted affect, criteria.
firstAid.location: Psychiatry: Cluster A, social detachment, restricted affect, criteria.

firstAid.detail: Psych: paranoid vs schizoid vs schizotypal distinguishing features.
firstAid.location: Psych: paranoid vs schizoid vs schizotypal distinguishing features.

firstAid.detail: Psych: Cluster B/C disorders and their core features for contrast.
firstAid.location: Psych: Cluster B/C disorders and their core features for contrast.

memoryHook: schizoiD = Distant by choice, Dull affect, no Delusions. He LIKES being alone - that's the tell.
